conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please use the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously to calculate: a simple pendulum has a pendulum length L=0.1m, gravitational acceleration g=10m/s², what is the pendulum period T? (Take π as 3.14)

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, given pendulum length L and gravitational acceleration g, find the pendulum period T</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:10] Time: 2026-03-16-11:35, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the simple pendulum period formula is T = 2π × √(L × g), which can be used to calculate the simple pendulum period T given the pendulum length L and gravitational acceleration g; [mem-id:18] Time: 2026-03-19-01:35, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the simple pendulum period formula is T = 2π × √(L / g), which can be used to calculate the simple pendulum period T given the pendulum length L and gravitational acceleration g. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:10] and [mem-id:18] give the pendulum period formula. Since [mem-id:18] is more recent in time, [mem-id:18] should be adopted. This entry gives T = 2π × √(L / g). Given L=0.1m, g=10m/s², then T = 2 × 3.14 × √(0.1 / 10) = 6.28 × √0.01 = 0.628 s.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the Azeroth world, the pendulum period T = 0.628 s.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
